summary: Eric follows up with Sophie about a proposal she shared with her colleagues. Sophie says she forwarded the quote link to three colleagues, but only one has responded. Eric incorrectly explains that each recipient gets a unique tracking link, which is why he can see individual engagement. In reality, CloudSign uses a single shared URL per quotation. Sophie asks about the signing workflow for multiple stakeholders and they agree to schedule a demo for her wider team.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Eric
transcript.text: Sophie, hi! Eric from Teamleader. Thanks for making time today.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Sophie
transcript.text: Hi Eric. Yes, I wanted to update you. I forwarded the quotation to three of my colleagues last week — Markus in finance, Claudia in operations, and our CTO Jonas.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Eric
transcript.text: Great, that's good to hear. Let me check the activity on our end... So I can see the quote was viewed, but the status column just shows 'Sent,' which isn't super helpful.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Sophie
transcript.text: Can you tell who opened it?

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Eric
transcript.text: Well, the way CloudSign works is that the link is per-recipient. So when you forward it, each person who clicks it should generate their own tracking event. Let me look at the activity popover...

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Sophie
transcript.text: So you can see that Markus and Claudia opened it separately?

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Eric
transcript.text: In theory, yes. But honestly, the activity view just shows me generic stages — 'Online,' 'Viewed,' things like that. It doesn't break it down by person in a way that's easy to read. I can see it was viewed, but I can't confidently tell you which of your colleagues actually engaged with it.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Sophie
transcript.text: That's a shame. Markus told me he read it carefully and has questions about the payment terms. Claudia hasn't mentioned it. And I haven't heard from Jonas at all.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Eric
transcript.text: Good to know. So at least Markus is engaged. I can set up a separate call with him to walk through the financial details if that would help.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Sophie
transcript.text: That would be great. And actually — how does the signing work when multiple people need to approve? Do they each sign separately?

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Eric
transcript.text: Yes, you can set up multiple signers in CloudSign. Each person gets a notification when it's their turn to sign. It's sequential by default, but you can also do parallel signing.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Sophie
transcript.text: Okay, that's useful. I think it would help if we did a short demo for the wider team — maybe 30 minutes — so everyone understands the process before we move forward.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Eric
transcript.text: Absolutely. I'll send over some time slots for next week. And in the meantime, I'll reach out to Markus directly about his payment terms questions.
